creator: Spectral Galileo
executive_summary: US Navy Hormuz blockade (confirmed Apr 12) remains the dominant macro event: WTI crude surged +9% to $105.28. Enterprise SaaS sector reached historic oversold extremes — NOW (RSI=17.1, fwdPE=16.5), CRM (RSI=22.2, fwdPE=11.1), MSFT (RSI=39.5) — creating a rare convergence of technical and fundamental opportunity. ETH leads crypto on relative strength (+6.2% 7d) with Glamsterdam upgrade in testing and BlackRock cutting ETF fees. Today's 8-pick portfolio balances: SaaS value recovery (NOW, CRM, MSFT), energy geopolitical play (CVX), growth AI (GOOGL, QCOM), defensive compounder (V), and crypto DCA (ETH). Earnings cluster Apr 22–May 1 provides multiple re-rating catalysts. Previous call accuracy: 7/8 correct (87.5%), with CVX +14.97% as standout winner on Iran thesis.

macro_environment.summary: Iran Hormuz blockade confirmed Apr 12 — US Navy deployed after Islamabad talks collapsed. WTI crude +9% to $105. Gold -2% paradoxically as Hormuz-driven inflation pushes rate-cut expectations back (CPI 3.3%, Fed Funds 3.75%). VIX=19.23 not in panic territory. F&G divergence: alt.me=16 (Extreme Fear) vs CMC=45 (Neutral) — different methodologies. Q1 earnings season opens: NOW (Apr 22), V (Apr 28), MSFT/GOOGL/QCOM (Apr 29), CVX (May 1). SPY RSI=67.8 — broad market elevated despite geopolitical shock.
macro_environment.key_event: US Navy Hormuz blockade — ~20% global oil supply disrupted, no near-term resolution

historical_accuracy.market_note: Iran Hormuz blockade directly validated the CVX energy thesis (+14.97%). SaaS repricing (CRM -27.66%) was the main miss — sector-wide multiple compression accelerated beyond model. 7/8 picks flat or positive.

risk_adjusted_picks.name: ServiceNow
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: RSI=17.1 — most extreme oversold in the entire 55-ticker universe. fwdPE=16.54x for a business with 77.5% gross margins, 37.3% FCF margin, and 20.7% revenue growth. This combination has historically preceded 30-50% mean reversions. Earnings catalyst in 8 days (Apr 22) with 4-quarter consecutive beat streak. $4.95B annual FCF generation. Market cap $86.8B at 52-week low ($83). Risk defined: $11 to stop vs $67 to target.

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Chevron
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: Iran Hormuz blockade is the single biggest supply shock since 2022. ~20% of global oil disrupted with no diplomatic resolution in sight. CVX was priced for $75-80 oil — with WTI at $105+ FCF will surge 30%+. RSI=34.9 remains in oversold territory despite +14.97% yesterday. Earnings May 1 will show material FCF revision upward. Energy thesis validated; extend hold.

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Microsoft
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: RSI=39.5 at key SMA200 support. Azure AI EPS beat +34% last quarter. fwdPE=19.7x for 40% net margins, $53.6B FCF, and 16.7% revenue growth — historically cheap for MSFT quality tier. Earnings Apr 29 with 4-quarter beat streak and Azure AI acceleration. Lowest-risk large-cap tech entry in universe. Goldman, Bernstein, BNP all Outperform. Avg analyst PT $587 (+58% upside).

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Salesforce
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: RSI=22.2 at new 52-week low ($163.52 floor). fwdPE=11.08x for a 39.4% FCF margin business — the cheapest large-cap SaaS in the universe on FCF yield. Agentforce AI platform is a genuine differentiated enterprise catalyst. 4-quarter beat streak. Average analyst PT $273 = +66% upside. The previous DCA call repriced -27.66%; at $164 the risk/reward is now superior to the original entry.

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Alphabet
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: RSI=60.1 in uptrend with MACD bullish — only Mag-7 member combining uptrend, non-overbought RSI, and earnings catalyst. fwdPE=23.62 with +40% EPS surprise last quarter. Earnings Apr 29 — Google Cloud AI and ad revenue poised to beat again. Best Mag-7 risk/reward after MSFT. Support at $308 SMA50 provides defined floor.

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Visa
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: 97.8% gross margins, 53.2% FCF margin — best capital efficiency in S&P 500. fwdPE=20.93 inexpensive for Visa quality. RSI=52 near support $295. Earnings Apr 28. Provides defensive ballast against tech volatility. Cross-border volume benefits from geopolitical travel disruption. Lowest-risk pick in portfolio — suitable as core quality anchor.

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Qualcomm
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: Cheapest semiconductor in coverage: fwdPE=11.55x, EV/EBITDA=10x. MACD turning bullish near $124 support. On-device AI is multi-year underappreciated catalyst — Snapdragon powers every premium Android phone and AI PC entering market in 2026. Earnings Apr 29. Automotive chip revenue +50% YoY. Deep value in world's most important AI silicon aside from NVDA.

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Ethereum
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: Best relative crypto performer: +6.2% 7d vs BTC -3.5% 24h. 85% community bullish on CoinGecko. Glamsterdam network upgrade in advanced testing. BlackRock cutting ETHA fees — institutional commitment signal. ETF AUM $13B growing. AI agent settlement layer narrative gaining institutional traction. RSI=65.8 not yet overbought. DCA: Tramo 1 at $2,196 (20%), Tramo 2 at $2,000 (40%), Tramo 3 at $1,850 (40%). Stop on full position below $1,800.

cross_sector_insights.stocks: SaaS sector at extreme oversold creates once-in-cycle entry: NOW (RSI 17.1) and CRM (RSI 22.2) are historically cheap on FCF/margin. Energy surging on Hormuz with CVX still not overbought. Mag-7 diverging — MSFT and GOOGL are highest conviction with clustered earnings catalysts Apr 28-29.
cross_sector_insights.crypto: Iran risk-off creating DCA entry opportunity. ETH outperforms on Glamsterdam upgrade + BlackRock ETF commitment + AI settlement layer narrative. BTC holding $70K but sentiment fragile (alt.me F&G=16). Prefer ETH over BTC for new capital.
cross_sector_insights.materials: Oil (CL +9%) is clear materials winner. CVX is the equity proxy for Hormuz play. Gold paradoxically declining—Hormuz inflation → delayed rate cuts = gold headwind via rate channel. Silver extended at -40% from ATH. Materials exposure captured through CVX.
